rulings.Case Title: RICHARD JOHNSON ET AL VS. INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant Moore Dry Dock Company's motion to dismiss the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that North Dakota is a suitable alternative forum because it did not show that all defendants are subject to jurisdiction there. (See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744; American Cemwood Corp. v. American Home Assurance Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 431.) Defendant's alternate motion to stay the action is denied. The balance of private and public interest factors makes it just that the litigation remain in California. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30a.m., not 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD JOHNSON ET AL VS. INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Owens-Illinois, Inc.S Joinder In Defendant Moore Dry Dock Companys Motion To Stay Or Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant Owens-Illinois, Inc.'s joinder in Moore Dry Dock Company's motion to dismiss or stay on grounds of forum non conveniens is off calendar as moot because the underlying motion remains on calendar and will be ruled upon. = (503/TLJ)
